content_en:
The figs and apples of Halwan are known for their quality, sweetness and delicious taste. The figs are appreciated all over the world and are known as the ‘Queen of Figs.’ Since this city is situated ina valley, the beautiful streams present a scenic beauty of spring.

The Muslim army conquered Halwan after they had taken Jalula’. When the Iranian Emperor heard of the coming of the Muslims he fled from Halwan to Isphahan and found refuge there. But he was fated to keep on fleeing from every place.

Nahawind is a huge and ancient city. Great scholar Yaqut Hamawi, in his book, Ma‘jam Al-Baldén has given detailed information about the

“If Nu’man bin Magran Mazni <% is martyred, Huthaifah bin Yamman should take over as Commander. If jhe too is killed then Jareer bin “Abdullah % should take over the responsibility. {f he martyred, then Mughirah bin Sha’bah t+ 4s 2, should take command of the army. After him Ash‘ath bin Qais .5 is to perform the duties of the commander.”

The Iranian had taken refuge in the fort and dug trenches all around Nahawind for protection, so the Muslim army besieged the city. Since the Iranian were not coming out to confront them, the Commander-inChief, Nu’man bin Maqran Mazni +, finally called for a meeting his most experienced generals. Among them were brave men such as ‘Amr bin Muthna, ‘Amr bin Ma‘adi Karb and Talihah bin Khawaled Liye a re

They all suggested that a few Mujahideen should be sent into the city under the leadership of an experienced general. When the [ranians attacked them they should race back. This would boost the courage of the Jranians and they would set out in a chase. Once they came out of the city, the Muslim army could launch a powerful attack. These Mujahideen who were to thus play on their lives were led by Qa‘qa’ bin ‘Amr . He, displaying great courage and daring, entered the city of Nahawind with lightening speed. When the Iranian army saw this unexpected threat they panicked; then they plucked up their courage and got ready to fight. When they advanced forward to battle with the invaders, Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr +48 and his men, in accordance with their plan, zoomed around and started to race back. Thus encouraged by what they saw as a retreat, the Iranian army came out into the battlefield. The whole of the Muslim army collectively made a powerful attack. A fierce battle followed, and in no time the field of Jihad was red with the blood of those killed from the two armies.

The Commander-in-Chief, Nu‘man bin Magran Mazni #%, exhibiting extraordinary bravery was martyred. When the Iranian Commanderin-Chief, Ferozan, saw the battle was at a critical juncture, he fled the field and started to climb up a mountain. But Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr 2, displaying great daring, gave chase, caught up with him and finally killed him.

This great enviable and heroic Mujahid, who was said to equal more than a thousand ordinary soldiers, immersed in his deep love for Allah 3 and the Prophet 3, had the honour of killing three of the enemy Generals in the battle of Qadissiyah. He was an experienced warrior, who in every battle with the Iranians terrified the enemy with his military prowess; he never craved for wealth or amassed it and never lusted after political power or worldly status. In the Battles of Jamal and Safain he was the ambassador of ‘Ali bin Abi Talib 4 and in these two battles his sword remained in its scabbard. He was a sympathetic Mujahid who after spending the last days of an exemplary and enviable life in the city of Kufa finally met with his Creator.

“Come back to your Lord, well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him)!” (89:28)

The friendship of Khalid bin Waleed and Sharjeel bin Hasanah Lge St ee performed a great role in the exaltation and eminence of Islam.” (Historians)

This youth came to be known in the annals of history as Sharjeel bin Hasanah .#. He was the son of ‘Abdullah bin Mata’ bin ’Amr bin Kundah. His mother was named Hasanah. He was an infant in his mother’s lap when his father passed away. After passing the period of her ‘Iddat’ his mother married Sufyan bin Mua’mmar Ansari. This is why his name rather than his father’s name knew by him mother. His mother was from the tribe of Banu ‘Adul who were inhabitants of Bahrain and aliies of the Banu Zahra. This respectable lady also had the honour of migrating to Ethiopia with her son Sharjeel 4.

When the benefactor of Mankind, first became the Messenger of Allah 38, Sharjeel bin Hasanah 4 was under the guardianship of his

stepfather, Sufyan bin Mua’mmar Ansari and being brought up by him. By his mother’s second marriage he had two brothers, Khalid bin Sufyan and Janadah bin Sufyan, who were born soon after each other.

When Prophet Muhammad # proclaimed himself as the Messenger of Allah, both his parents had the honour to be among the first to accept Islam. Thus from his very childhood he had the chance to grow up in Islamic environment. During. this critical period the newly converted Muslims underwent great hardships and suffering. They were ground in the mills of cruelty and oppression. Their naked bodies were subjected to the whiplash and they were dragged naked across the pebbled sands of the burning desert.

They were made to lie down on burning fires and heavy stones were laid across their chests. All new method of torture was tested callously and cruelly on them.

Doubtless in the context of the spreading of the Message this was the most tragic and terrible period in the history of Islam. It chills the spine of any person who turns the pages of this dark period filled with blood and gore. Which cruelty was left untried on these unarmed Muslims? Which torment was not tested on these innocent and pure bodies of Muslims? Which punishment did their bodies not taste?

Tt was only they whose lives had been blessed and brightened directly by the radiance of the Messenger’s personality. Whose hearts had been illuminated by the True Faith, who could call out ina trance, as it were, the Name of Allah 3 even as they were being tugged naked across the burning pebbles of the sandy desert or lying down on live coals. When the sufferings and, agony of the Quraish had exceeded all limits, these obedient and noble Companions received orders from the Prophet 3 to migrate towards Ethiopia. The Emperor Najashi of Ethiopia was wellknown in the Arab world for his justice and hospitality. Thus the very first caravan of oppressed migrants in the history of Islam set out for Ethiopia. It comprised eleven men and four women. They were provided accommodation and their days and nights passed in peace and tranquillity. One day they received news that there was peace in Makkah at last and Muslims were no longer subject to persecution. Their love for their motherland compelled them to return. When they reached there, their hearts filled with love and joy, but they found that

the news they had received was not correct. Things had not changed in any way, in fact the Quraish was targeting the Muslims even more and they were being inflicted with worse abuse and torment. Tired of this constant subjugation and tyranny, the Muslims were forced to migrate for a second time. This time round besides the women and children there were eighty men, who set out for Ethiopia. And this time Sharjeel (4) was with his family among the members of the caravan.

They remained in Ethiopia till such time as the Prophet # migrated to Al-Madinah. When this news reached the Muslim migrants in Ethiopia that he had bid goodbye to Makkah and had moved permanently to Al-Madinah and settled there, they were delighted. Gradually they also started moving there.

In the year 9 Hijrah the Prophet 38, Commanding the Muslim forces set out for Tabook to grapple the problems. After his return he appointed Khalid bin Waleed % as the Commander of four hundred warriors to go to Domatul-Jandal to subjugate the Banu Kundah tribe. Their chieftain, Akidar bin ‘Abdul-Malik, was a Christian.

Khalid bin Waleed 4 asked:

“O Messenger of Allah 3% how can I conquer such a powerful enemy with such a small army?”

The Prophet 38 replied:
content_id:
Buah tin dan apel Halwan terkenal karena kualitas, kemanisan, dan kelezatan rasanya. Buah tin tersebut dihargai di seluruh dunia dan dikenal sebagai 'Ratu Buah Tin.' Karena kota ini terletak di sebuah lembah, aliran sungai yang indah menghadirkan keindahan pemandangan musim semi.

Pasukan Muslim menaklukkan Halwan setelah mereka merebut Jalula'. Ketika Kaisar Iran mendengar kedatangan kaum Muslimin, ia melarikan diri dari Halwan ke Isphahan dan berlindung di sana. Namun ia ditakdirkan untuk terus melarikan diri dari setiap tempat.

Nahawind adalah kota besar dan kuno. Ulama besar Yaqut Hamawi, dalam kitabnya, Ma'jam Al-Buldan telah memberikan informasi rinci tentang

"Jika Nu'man bin Maqran Mazni radhiyallahu 'anhu gugur sebagai syahid, Huthaifah bin Yamman harus mengambil alih sebagai Panglima. Jika ia juga terbunuh maka Jareer bin 'Abdullah radhiyallahu 'anhu harus mengambil alih tanggung jawab tersebut. Jika ia gugur, maka Mughirah bin Sya'bah radhiyallahu 'anhu harus memimpin pasukan. Setelahnya Asy'ats bin Qais radhiyallahu 'anhu yang menjalankan tugas sebagai panglima."

Pasukan Iran telah berlindung di dalam benteng dan menggali parit-parit di sekeliling Nahawind untuk perlindungan, sehingga pasukan Muslim mengepung kota tersebut. Karena pasukan Iran tidak keluar untuk menghadapi mereka, Panglima Tertinggi, Nu'man bin Maqran Mazni radhiyallahu 'anhu, akhirnya mengadakan pertemuan dengan para jenderal paling berpengalamannya. Di antara mereka terdapat pejuang-pejuang pemberani seperti 'Amr bin Mutsanna, 'Amr bin Ma'adi Karb dan Thalihah bin Khuwailid radhiyallahu 'anhum.

Mereka semua menyarankan agar beberapa mujahidin dikirim ke dalam kota di bawah pimpinan seorang jenderal berpengalaman. Ketika pasukan Iran menyerang mereka, mereka harus bergegas mundur. Ini akan meningkatkan keberanian pasukan Iran dan mereka akan mengejar. Begitu mereka keluar dari kota, pasukan Muslim dapat melancarkan serangan yang dahsyat. Para mujahidin yang mempertaruhkan nyawa mereka ini dipimpin oleh Qa'qa' bin 'Amr radhiyallahu 'anhu. Ia, dengan menunjukkan keberanian dan keberanian luar biasa, memasuki kota Nahawind dengan kecepatan kilat. Ketika pasukan Iran melihat ancaman tak terduga ini, mereka panik; kemudian mereka mengumpulkan keberanian dan bersiap untuk bertempur. Ketika mereka maju ke depan untuk bertempur dengan penyerbu, Qa'qa' bin 'Amr radhiyallahu 'anhu dan anak buahnya, sesuai dengan rencana mereka, berputar dan mulai mundur dengan cepat. Dengan demikian, terdorong oleh apa yang mereka anggap sebagai mundurnya musuh, pasukan Iran keluar ke medan perang. Seluruh pasukan Muslim secara bersama-sama melancarkan serangan yang dahsyat. Pertempuran sengit pun terjadi, dan dalam waktu singkat medan jihad memerah oleh darah mereka yang terbunuh dari kedua pasukan.

Panglima Tertinggi, Nu'man bin Maqran Mazni radhiyallahu 'anhu, yang menunjukkan keberanian luar biasa, gugur sebagai syahid. Ketika Panglima Iran, Ferozan, melihat pertempuran berada di titik kritis, ia melarikan diri dari medan perang dan mulai mendaki gunung. Namun Qa'qa' bin 'Amr radhiyallahu 'anhu, dengan menunjukkan keberanian besar, mengejarnya, menyusulnya dan akhirnya membunuhnya.

Mujahid besar yang patut diteladani dan heroik ini, yang dikatakan setara dengan lebih dari seribu prajurit biasa, yang tenggelam dalam kecintaannya yang mendalam kepada Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala dan Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, mendapat kehormatan membunuh tiga jenderal musuh dalam Pertempuran Qadisiyyah. Ia adalah pejuang berpengalaman, yang dalam setiap pertempuran melawan Iran menggetarkan musuh dengan kehebatan militernya; ia tidak pernah mendambakan kekayaan atau menumpuknya dan tidak pernah menginginkan kekuasaan politik atau kedudukan duniawi. Dalam Pertempuran Jamal dan Shiffin ia menjadi duta 'Ali bin Abi Thalib radhiyallahu 'anhu dan dalam kedua pertempuran itu pedangnya tetap dalam sarungnya. Ia adalah seorang mujahid yang penuh simpati yang setelah menghabiskan hari-hari terakhir kehidupannya yang teladan dan patut diteladani di kota Kufah akhirnya berjumpa dengan Penciptanya.

"Kembalilah kepada Tuhanmu dengan hati yang ridha dan diridhai-Nya!" (QS. Al-Fajr: 28)

"Persahabatan Khalid bin Waleed dan Syarjeel bin Hasanah radhiyallahu 'anhuma memainkan peranan besar dalam ketinggian dan kemuliaan Islam." (Para Sejarawan)

Pemuda ini dikenal dalam catatan sejarah sebagai Syarjeel bin Hasanah radhiyallahu 'anhu. Ia adalah putra 'Abdullah bin Mata' bin 'Amr bin Kundah. Ibunya bernama Hasanah. Ia masih bayi dalam pangkuan ibunya ketika ayahnya wafat. Setelah melewati masa 'iddah, ibunya menikah dengan Sufyan bin Mu'ammar Anshari. Itulah sebabnya ia dikenal dengan nama ibunya dan bukan nama ayahnya. Ibunya berasal dari suku Banu 'Adul yang merupakan penduduk Bahrain dan sekutu Banu Zahra. Wanita terhormat ini juga mendapat kehormatan berhijrah ke Ethiopia bersama putranya Syarjeel radhiyallahu 'anhu.

Ketika pembawa kebaikan bagi umat manusia pertama kali menjadi Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, Syarjeel bin Hasanah radhiyallahu 'anhu berada di bawah pengasuhan

ayah tirinya, Sufyan bin Mu'ammar Anshari dan dibesarkan olehnya. Dari pernikahan kedua ibunya ia memiliki dua saudara laki-laki, Khalid bin Sufyan dan Janadah bin Sufyan, yang lahir berdekatan satu sama lain.

Ketika Nabi Muhammad shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam menyatakan dirinya sebagai Rasulullah, kedua orang tuanya mendapat kehormatan menjadi orang-orang pertama yang memeluk Islam. Dengan demikian sejak kecil ia memiliki kesempatan untuk tumbuh dalam lingkungan Islami. Selama masa kritis ini, kaum Muslimin yang baru masuk Islam mengalami kesulitan dan penderitaan yang besar. Mereka digiling dalam penggilingan kekejaman dan penindasan. Tubuh mereka yang telanjang dicambuk dan mereka diseret telanjang melintasi kerikil pasir gurun yang membakar.

Mereka dipaksa berbaring di atas api yang menyala dan batu-batu berat diletakkan di atas dada mereka. Segala metode penyiksaan baru diujikan dengan kejam dan tanpa belas kasihan kepada mereka.

Tidak diragukan lagi dalam konteks penyebaran risalah ini adalah masa paling tragis dan mengerikan dalam sejarah Islam. Bulu kuduk siapa pun akan berdiri ketika membuka lembaran-lembaran masa kelam yang penuh dengan darah dan kengerian ini. Kekejaman apa yang tidak dicobakan kepada kaum Muslimin yang tak bersenjata ini? Siksaan apa yang tidak diujikan kepada tubuh-tubuh suci dan tak berdosa kaum Muslimin ini? Hukuman apa yang tidak dirasakan oleh tubuh mereka?

Hanya mereka yang kehidupannya telah diberkahi dan diterangi langsung oleh cahaya kepribadian Rasulullah. Yang hati mereka telah diterangi oleh Iman yang Benar, yang bisa menyerukan dalam keadaan seperti trance Nama Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala bahkan ketika mereka diseret telanjang melintasi kerikil panas gurun pasir yang membakar atau dibaringkan di atas bara api yang menyala. Ketika penderitaan dan kesengsaraan dari kaum Quraisy telah melampaui segala batas, para sahabat yang taat dan mulia ini menerima perintah dari Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam untuk berhijrah ke Ethiopia. Kaisar Najasyi dari Ethiopia terkenal di dunia Arab karena keadilan dan keramahtamahannya. Maka kafilah pertama para migran yang tertindas dalam sejarah Islam berangkat menuju Ethiopia. Kafilah itu terdiri dari sebelas pria dan empat wanita. Mereka disediakan tempat tinggal dan hari-hari serta malam-malam mereka berlalu dalam kedamaian dan ketenangan. Suatu hari mereka menerima kabar bahwa akhirnya ada perdamaian di Makkah dan kaum Muslimin tidak lagi mengalami penganiayaan. Kecintaan mereka terhadap tanah air memaksa mereka untuk kembali. Ketika mereka tiba di sana, dengan hati yang penuh cinta dan kegembiraan, mereka mendapati bahwa

kabar yang mereka terima tidaklah benar. Keadaan tidak berubah sama sekali, bahkan kaum Quraisy semakin mengincar kaum Muslimin dan mereka mengalami penyiksaan dan siksaan yang lebih parah. Lelah dengan penindasan dan kezaliman yang terus-menerus, kaum Muslimin terpaksa berhijrah untuk kedua kalinya. Kali ini selain wanita dan anak-anak terdapat delapan puluh pria, yang berangkat menuju Ethiopia. Dan kali ini Syarjeel radhiyallahu 'anhu bersama keluarganya termasuk di antara anggota kafilah tersebut.

Mereka tetap tinggal di Ethiopia hingga Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam berhijrah ke Madinah. Ketika kabar ini sampai kepada para migran Muslim di Ethiopia bahwa beliau telah meninggalkan Makkah dan pindah secara permanen ke Madinah serta menetap di sana, mereka sangat gembira. Secara bertahap mereka juga mulai berpindah ke sana.

Pada tahun 9 Hijriah, Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, memimpin pasukan Muslim berangkat menuju Tabuk untuk mengatasi permasalahan. Setelah kembali, beliau menunjuk Khalid bin Waleed radhiyallahu 'anhu sebagai Panglima empat ratus prajurit untuk pergi ke Dumatul Jandal untuk menundukkan suku Banu Kundah. Kepala suku mereka, Akidar bin 'Abdul Malik, adalah seorang Nasrani.

Khalid bin Waleed radhiyallahu 'anhu bertanya:

"Wahai Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bagaimana saya bisa menaklukkan musuh yang begitu kuat dengan pasukan yang begitu kecil?"

Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam menjawab:
content_ar: The figs and apples of Halwan are known for their quality, sweetness and delicious taste. The figs are appreciated all over the الدنيا and are known as the ‘Queen of Figs.’ بما أن this city is situated ina valley, the beautiful streams present a scenic beauty of spring. The مسلم الجيش conquered Halwan بعد they had taken Jalula’. عندما the Iranian Emperor heard of the coming of the المسلمون he fled from Halwan to Isphahan and found refuge there. But he was fated to keep on fleeing from every place. Nahawind is a huge and ancient city. Great scholar Yaqut Hamawi, in his الكتاب, Ma‘jam Al-Baldén has given detailed information about the “إذا Nu’man bin Magran Mazni <% is الشهيدed, Huthaifah bin Yamman should take over as القائد. إذا jhe أيضاً is killed then Jareer bin “Abdullah % should take over the المسؤولية. {f he الشهيدed, then Mughirah bin Sha’bah t+ 4s 2, should take command of the الجيش. بعد him Ash‘ath bin Qais .5 is to perform the duties of the القائد.” The Iranian had taken refuge in the fort and dug trenches all around Nahawind for protection, لذا the مسلم الجيش besieged the city. بما أن the Iranian were not coming out to confront them, the القائد-inChief, Nu’man bin Maqran Mazni +, وأخيراً called for a meeting his most experienced generals. Among them were brave men مثل ‘Amr bin Muthna, ‘Amr bin Ma‘adi Karb and Talihah bin Khawaled Liye a re They all suggested that a few المجاهدين should be sent into the city under the leadership of an experienced general. عندما the [ranians attacked them they should race back. This would boost the الشجاعة of the Jranians and they would set out in a chase. حين they came out of the city, the مسلم الجيش could launch a powerful attack. These المجاهدين who were to وهكذا play on their lives were led by Qa‘qa’ bin ‘Amr . He, displaying great الشجاعة and daring, entered the city of Nahawind with lightening speed. عندما the Iranian الجيش saw this unexpected threat they panicked; then they plucked up their الشجاعة and got ready to fight. عندما they advanced forward to المعركة with the invaders, Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr +48 and his men, in accordance with their plan, zoomed around and started to race back. وهكذا encouraged by what they saw as a retreat, the Iranian الجيش came out into the المعركةfield. The whole of the مسلم الجيش collectively made a powerful attack. A fierce المعركة followed, and in no time the field of الجهاد was red with the blood of those killed from the two armies. The القائد-in-Chief, Nu‘man bin Magran Mazni #%, exhibiting extraordinary bravery was الشهيدed. عندما the Iranian القائدin-Chief, Ferozan, saw the المعركة was at a البالغ الأهمية juncture, he fled the field and started to climb up a mountain. But Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr 2, displaying great daring, gave chase, caught up with him and وأخيراً killed him. This great enviable and heroic Mujahid, who was said to equal more than a thousand ordinary soldiers, immersed in his deep المحبة for الله 3 and النبي ﷺ 3, had the honour of killing three of the العدو Generals in the المعركة of Qadissiyah. He was an experienced warrior, who in every المعركة with the Iranians terrified the العدو with his military prowess; he never craved for الثروة or amassed it and never lusted بعد political power or worldly status. In the المعركةs of Jamal and Safain he was the ambassador of ‘علي bin Abi Talib 4 and in these two المعركةs his السيف remained in its scabbard. He was a sympathetic Mujahid who بعد spending the last days of an exemplary and enviable الحياة in the city of Kufa وأخيراً met with his Creator. “Come back to your Lord, well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him)!” (89:28) The friendship of Khalid bin Waleed and Sharjeel bin Hasanah Lge St ee performed a great role in the exaltation and eminence of الإسلام.” (Historians) This youth came to be known in the annals of history as Sharjeel bin Hasanah .#. He was the son of ‘Abdullah bin Mata’ bin ’Amr bin Kundah. His الأم was named Hasanah. He was an infant in his الأم’s lap عندما his الأب passed away. بعد passing the period of her ‘Iddat’ his الأم married Sufyan bin Mua’mmar Ansari. This is why his name rather than his الأب’s name knew by him الأم. His الأم was from the tribe of Banu ‘Adul who were inhabitants of Bahrain and aliies of the Banu Zahra. This respectable lady أيضاً had the honour of migrating to Ethiopia with her son Sharjeel 4. عندما the benefactor of Mankind, أولاً became الرسول ﷺ of الله 38, Sharjeel bin Hasanah 4 was under the guardianship of his stepfather, Sufyan bin Mua’mmar Ansari and being brought up by him. By his الأم’s ثانياً marriage he had two brothers, Khalid bin Sufyan and Janadah bin Sufyan, who were born soon بعد بعضهم بعضاً. عندما النبي محمد ﷺ # proclaimed himself as الرسول ﷺ of الله, both his الوالدان had the honour to be among the أولاً to accept الإسلام. وهكذا from his very childhood he had the chance to grow up in Islamic environment. خلال. this البالغ الأهمية period the newly converted المسلمون underwent great hardships and المعاناة. They were ground in the mills of cruelty and oppression. Their naked bodies were subjected to the whiplash and they were dragged naked across the pebbled sands of the burning desert. They were made to lie down on burning fires and heavy stones were laid across their chests. All new method of torture was tested callously and cruelly on them. Doubtless in the context of the spreading of the Message this was the most tragic and terrible period in the history of الإسلام. It chills the spine of any person who turns the pages of this dark period filled with blood and gore. Which cruelty was left untried on these unarmed المسلمون? Which torment was not tested on these innocent and pure bodies of المسلمون? Which العقاب did their bodies not taste? Tt was only they whose lives had been blessed and brightened directly by the radiance of الرسول ﷺ’s personality. Whose القلوب had been illuminated by the True الإيمان, who could call out ina trance, as it were, the Name of الله 3 even as they were being tugged naked across the burning pebbles of the sandy desert or lying down on live coals. عندما the sufferings and, agony of the Quraish had exceeded all limits, these obedient and noble الصحابة received orders from النبي ﷺ 3 to migrate towards Ethiopia. The Emperor Najashi of Ethiopia was wellknown in the Arab الدنيا for his العدل and الضيافة. وهكذا the very أولاً caravan of oppressed migrants in the history of الإسلام set out for Ethiopia. It comprised eleven men and four women. They were provided accommodation and their days and nights passed in peace and tranquillity. يوماً ما they received news that there was peace in مكة المكرمة at last and المسلمون were no longer subject to persecution. Their المحبة for their motherland compelled them to return. عندما they reached there, their القلوب filled with المحبة and joy, but they found that the news they had received was not correct. Things had not changed in any الطريق, in fact the Quraish was targeting the المسلمون even more and they were being inflicted with worse abuse and torment. Tired of this constant subjugation and tyranny, the المسلمون were forced to migrate for a ثانياً time. This time round بالإضافة إلى the women and الأولاد there were eighty men, who set out for Ethiopia. And this time Sharjeel (4) was with his الأسرة among the members of the caravan. They remained in Ethiopia till such time as النبي ﷺ # migrated to Al-المدينة المنورة. عندما this news reached the مسلم migrants in Ethiopia that he had bid goodbye to مكة المكرمة and had moved permanently to Al-المدينة المنورة and settled there, they were delighted. Gradually they أيضاً started moving there. In the year 9 Hijrah النبي ﷺ 38, Commanding the مسلم forces set out for Tabook to grapple the problems. بعد his return he appointed Khalid bin Waleed % as the القائد of four hundred warriors to go to Domatul-Jandal to subjugate the Banu Kundah tribe. Their chieftain, Akidar bin ‘Abdul-مالك, was a Christian. Khalid bin Waleed 4 asked: “O Messenger of الله 3% how can I conquer such a powerful العدو with such a small الجيش?” النبي ﷺ 38 replied:
